stem: A car's fuel efficiency is rated at 32 miles per gallon. What is this fuel efficiency, in kilometers per liter, rounded to the nearest tenth? (Use 1 mile = 1.609 kilometers and 1 gallon = 3.785 liters)
explanation:
The given rate is 32 miles per gallon. Both the distance unit (miles) and the volume unit (gallons) must be converted simultaneously — miles to kilometers and gallons to liters. The student must recognize the correct direction for each: multiply to convert miles to km (since 1 mile > 1 km, more km than miles), and divide to convert gallons to liters (since 1 gallon contains multiple liters).

Step 1 — Set up the unit chain:
$$\frac{32 \text{ mi}}{1 \text{ gal}} \times \frac{1.609 \text{ km}}{1 \text{ mi}} \times \frac{1 \text{ gal}}{3.785 \text{ L}}$$

Step 2 — Cancel units (mi cancels, gal cancels):
$$= \frac{32 \times 1.609}{3.785} \text{ km/L}$$

Step 3 — Calculate:
$$= \frac{51.488}{3.785} \approx 13.6 \text{ km/L}$$

The fuel efficiency is approximately $\boxed{13.6}$ km/L.

distractorRationale.A: Inverted distance conversion error: the student divides by 1.609 instead of multiplying, treating the conversion as miles per kilometer rather than kilometers per mile. Calculation: $32 \div 1.609 \div 3.785 = 19.888 \div 3.785 \approx 5.3$ km/L. This reflects confusion about which direction to apply the conversion factor when going from miles to kilometers.
distractorRationale.B: Wrong volume conversion factor error: the student uses 1 gallon = 4 liters (a rounded approximation) instead of 3.785 liters. Calculation: $32 \times 1.609 \div 4 = 51.488 \div 4 = 12.9$ km/L.
distractorRationale.C: Correct answer. Multiplies by 1.609 km/mi to convert the distance unit and divides by 3.785 L/gal to convert the volume unit.
distractorRationale.D: Swapped conversion factors error: the student multiplies by the volume conversion factor and divides by the distance conversion factor, reversing the roles of the two factors in the chain. Calculation: $32 \times 3.785 \div 1.609 = 121.12 \div 1.609 \approx 75.3$ km/L. This represents applying the gallon-to-liter ratio where the mile-to-km ratio belongs, and vice versa.
